1.description: Model fit. The panels plot for each variable the model-implied values (red dashed line) and compare them with the data (black solid line), including 95% confidence bounds (black dotted lines). The confidence bounds are based on the standard deviations of the annual time-series observations of percentile breakpoints. The figure illustrates the firm-size distribution (the top panel), the book-to-market ratio (BtM) distribution (the second panel), the investment (change in book value) distribution (the third panel), the monthly value-weighted alpha of each book-to-market decile portfolio (the fourth panel), as well as the relative market value of each book-to-market decile portfolio (the bottom panel). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Investment distortions. The graph plots a histogram of the difference between the distorted and undistorted expected investment rate in each state, where the expected investment rate refers to(i+−i−)in our model. The histogram is computed based on the stationary distribution of the initial distorted economy, the parameters of which are detailed in TableII. The undistorted expected investment rates in each state are computed by solving the model with the same parameters, except that we setαi=0∀i. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Aggregate efficiency gain as a function of the value spread. This graph plots the aggregategainmeasure in equation29for different economies that are indexed by their value spread. The value spread is measured as the difference between the alpha of the top decile portfolio and the alpha of the bottom decile portfolio. We vary the value spread by increasing the range of the α-process while maintaining a value-weighted alpha of zero in each economy. Starting with the α-process parameters specified in TableII, we increase or decrease the value for α3. The values of all alpha states are then shifted by an equal amount to ensure that the value-weighted average alpha is still zero.

4.description: Gains and Tobin'sq. The blue solid line plots the average relative gain (left vertical axis) for a typical firm with a given Tobin'sq(horizontal axis) from moving to undistorted investment policies. The orange squares plot a histogram of how the aggregate valuegainin29is distributed across firms (right vertical axis) with different Tobin'sq. The parameters of the economy are detailed in TableII. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Investment-alpha relationship. The graph plots the CAPM alphas of investment-sorted decile portfolios in the model (red dashed line) and compares them with the data (black solid line), including 95% confidence bounds (black dotted lines). The parameters of the economy are detailed in TableII. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Alpha process persistence and aggregate gains. The graphs illustrate the effects of changing the persistence of the α-process on the aggregate efficiencygainand on the value spread. We multiply the transition rates(hα+,hα−)of the baseline parameterization by a factor [0.5, 2]. The values of all alpha states are then shifted by an equal amount to ensure that the value-weighted average alpha is still zero in each economy. The other parameters of the economy are detailed in TableII. The value spread is measured as the difference between the alpha of the top decile portfolio and the alpha of the bottom decile portfolio.
